description: CANDIDATE / DRAFT, non-normative (RUN-LEX-01 EXP-LEX-04). An OPTIONAL freshness/status layer ON TOP OF the CID-First Attestation Specification (badge.blue) — it changes nothing in that spec. ONE signed tree head per issuer era/epoch (CT / RFC-9162 / RFC-6962 shape) over KEYED commitments (HMAC over credential CIDs), leaves in canonical byte-ascending order (mint order structurally absent), the leaf count, and the era's superseded set + its root. The head record is signed with the SAME CID-first ECDSA the base spec defines (the issuer signs plain_cid(head)); confirmation is a holder-STAPLED inclusion proof verified by a pure function with ZERO callback to the issuer. Naming is EL OC-2 (status vs freshness vs staple) — proposed for community.lexicon.* via the WG process, nothing lands there by our hand alone.

epochNo.description: Contiguous from 1; a gap fails a head audit.

eraAnchor.description: The governance fact opening this era. Era references are contiguous.

leafCount.description: Era-cumulative leaf count — epoch-grain volume, a count never a partition.

root.description: RFC-6962 Merkle root over the era's keyed commitments in byte-ascending order.

superseded.description: The era's superseded (revoked) keyed commitments; a superseded credential cannot staple a fresh-head proof.

supersededRoot.description: Digest of the canonical superseded-set encoding; inside the signed head so the published set cannot be quietly thinned.
